items.title: Jim Cramer Says He Doesn’t “Want to Buy CAT”
items.description: Caterpillar Inc. (NYSE:CAT) is one of the stocks Jim Cramer commented on. Cramer mentioned the company during the episode and said: “I like to look for stories that have been red hot, been, okay, that have suddenly cooled purely because of guilt by association. This morning, Morgan Stanley put the wood to Caterpillar, downgrading the […]

items.title: CAT Down 5% Since Q2 Earnings Miss: Buy, Sell or Hold the Stock?
items.description: Caterpillar's Q2 saw falling revenues and earnings but a rebound in volumes and an upbeat 2025 revenue outlook keep long-term growth hopes alive.

items.title: Is Most-Watched Stock Caterpillar Inc. (CAT) Worth Betting on Now?
items.description: Zacks.com users have recently been watching Caterpillar (CAT) quite a bit. Thus, it is worth knowing the facts that could determine the stock's prospects.

items.title: Berkshire Hathaway 13F Preview: Did Buffett Trim Apple—And Will The Mystery Stock Be Revealed?
items.description: Warren Buffett has requested confidentiality in a new Berkshire Hathaway position. Will investors find out the stock Thursday?

items.title: Qatar Construction Equipment Market Report 2025-2030 | Government Projects Worth $19 Billion Set to Boost Qatar Construction Equipment Demand
items.description: The Qatar Construction Equipment Market is projected to grow from 4,039 units in 2024 to 5,032 units by 2030, at a CAGR of 3.73%, driven by massive investments in public infrastructure. Key initiatives like the Doha Metro and Lusail city urban development are catalysts for market expansion. The material handling segment leads, with market giants Liebherr and JCB at the forefront.
